headline: Airbus Wins Major Order from Air China to Firm Regional Hold

headline: Boeing Targets 100-Jet SMBC Deal as Airbus Battles for Major Order

headline: Boeing: Global Commercial Fleet Will Top 50,000 Airplanes in 20 Years as Passenger Traffic Doubles
summary: Boeing [NYSE: BA] projects near-term disruptions will not meaningfully affect long-term aviation industry growth with demand for air travel set to double over the next 20 years. The global commercial airplane fleet is expected to grow nearly 80% to more than 50,000 airplanes by 2045 as airlines and cargo operators add capacity.

content.title: Boeing: Global Commercial Fleet Will Top 50,000 Airplanes in 20 Years as Passenger Traffic Doubles
content.summary: Boeing [NYSE: BA] projects near-term disruptions will not meaningfully affect long-term aviation industry growth with demand for air travel set to double over the next 20 years. The global commercial airplane fleet is expected to grow nearly 80% to more than 50,000 airplanes by 2045 as airlines and cargo operators add capacity.

thumbnail.caption: Boeing released its 2026 Commercial Market Outlook (CMO), forecasting that operators will need nearly 44,000 new airplanes to support sustained air travel demand and air cargo expansion over the next 20 years.

headline: Boeing Forecasts $4.9 Trillion Commercial Aviation Support and Services Market and Demand for more than 2.4 Million New Aviation Personnel Over 20 Years
summary: Boeing [NYSE: BA] today released its comprehensive 20-year outlook for the commercial aviation services market, the Boeing Services Market Outlook, and the Boeing Pilot & Technician Outlook, focused on projected workforce requirements. Over the next two decades, Boeing forecasts a $4.9 trillion support and services market, alongside a demand for over 2.4 million new commercial aviation professionals.

content.title: Boeing Forecasts $4.9 Trillion Commercial Aviation Support and Services Market and Demand for more than 2.4 Million New Aviation Personnel Over 20 Years
content.summary: Boeing [NYSE: BA] today released its comprehensive 20-year outlook for the commercial aviation services market, the Boeing Services Market Outlook, and the Boeing Pilot & Technician Outlook, focused on projected workforce requirements. Over the next two decades, Boeing forecasts a $4.9 trillion support and services market, alongside a demand for over 2.4 million new commercial aviation professionals.
